domain: Saharan Tuareg azalai salt caravan (2000-4000 camels; biannual cross-Sahara Timbuktu↔Taoudenni; multi-stop oasis-to-oasis salt-for-X trading; cumulative mixed-portfolio outcome)
specific_mechanism: Long-horizon multi-stop traversal trading single cargo for local commodities at each waypoint; accumulates mixed portfolio; resource regeneration at oases.
llm_application: AZALAI-CURRICULUM: K-oasis sequential fine-tuning curriculum where (i) model traverses K domain-specific datasets in fixed order, (ii) at each domain it 'trades' some general-capability magnitude for domain-specific weight updates (via a partial-overwrite LoRA with explicit forget-rate per layer), (iii) every other oasis is a REGULARIZATION OASIS using a frozen-prior penalty to refresh foundation knowledge (water refuel), (iv) final model carries explicit portfolio of K domain specializations + foundation. Distinct from sequential FT (no portfolio tracking, no regen) and from multi-task FT (parallel, not sequential).
novelty_claim: Standard sequential FT loses prior domains via catastrophic forgetting. Multi-task FT trains in parallel. EWC/LwF preserve via regularization but no explicit per-domain trade-portfolio. AZALAI-CURRICULUM differs by ALTERNATING SPECIALIZATION + REGULARIZATION OASES + EXPLICIT FORGET-RATE PER LAYER + PORTFOLIO TRACKING — designed for long-horizon multi-domain accumulation without forgetting.
